name: crra_tstat_formula_pin
purpose: Pins compute_crra_eu_tstat to the one-sample t-stat formula t = mean(U)/(sd(U)/sqrt(T)) and rejects the H-6 Sortino-form category error t = mean(U)*sqrt(T). Fixture values are derived from a deterministic arithmetic progression U = [a + b*i for i in range(T)] with a=0.01, b=0.005, T=8, yielding sd(U) far from 1.0 so the two formulae diverge by construction.
provenance: Non-circular: values derived by closed-form arithmetic; no autotuner/SUT output was used. Recipe: U[i] = 0.01 + 0.005*i for i in 0..7. mean = a + b*(T-1)/2 = 0.01 + 0.005*3.5 = 0.0275. sample_var (Bessel ddof=1) = b^2 * T*(T+1)/12 ... = statistics.stdev(U)^2. sd_U = sqrt(sample_var). t_correct = mean_U / (sd_U / sqrt(T)). t_sortino_form = mean_U * sqrt(T) -- the H-6 wrong form for a mean-valued objective.

recipe.U_formula: U[i] = a + b*i for i in range(T)
recipe.t_sortino_form_formula: mean_U * sqrt(T)  -- WRONG for a mean-valued objective; the H-6 category error

expected.discriminating_note: sd(U)=0.01225, which is not 1.0. The ratio t_correct/t_sortino_form = 81.6, meaning a Sortino-form SUT cannot accidentally pass the negative-identity assertion (the two values differ by >8000%). A SUT using mean(U)*sqrt(T) will match t_sortino_form and fail test_crra_eu_tstat_rejects_sortino_form_category_error.

tolerance_notes.positive_identity: rel=1e-9 -- both sides are pure double-precision arithmetic on a fixed input; only last-bit rounding is allowed
tolerance_notes.negative_identity: rel=1e-3 (loose) -- deliberately catches any near-Sortino-form implementation, even with a constant fudge factor
